commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d0f13a1d64d9feaca6c14abaf4b4b6b75b3c2029)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads cron wake `sessionKey` and `agentId` values through the cron agent tool, gateway wake validation/handler, cron service wake path, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main's cron tool wake path still sends only `{ mode, text }`, while the gateway/cron service already supports `sessionKey`, so the missing origin handoff is clear without running tests.
commands.review_followup: This needs contributor or maintainer rebase plus external real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's real setup evidence.

target.title: fix(cron): capture originating session/agent on the cron wake tool call
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** Cron `wake` MCP tool forwards only `{mode, text}` to the gateway. With no `sessionKey` / `agentId` reaching `context.cron.wake()`, every wake enqueues a system event against the heartbeat / main default — so wakes scheduled from a non-main session silently route to the wrong conversation lane.
- **Why it matters.** On CLI runtimes the fired wake spawns a fresh session and burns tokens generating output the caller has no way to route back. On embedded the wake fires the agent's default session, not the originating one. Broken across every channel.
- **What changed.** Wake tool resolves `opts.agentSessionKey` via the existing `resolveInternalSessionKey` / `resolveSessionAgentId` pattern (mirrors the `add` action at L569-583) and threads `sessionKey` + `agentId` through `WakeParamsSchema` → gateway handler → `context.cron.wake` → `enqueueSystemEvent` + `requestHeartbeatNow`. Explicit params take precedence over inferred values.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** `WakeParamsSchema` still permits `additionalProperties: true`. Pre-existing call sites that omit origin still route to the dep's default (backwards compatible). `delivery.{channel,threadId,topicId}` capture on the job and CLI runner `useResume`-on-cron-trigger are deliberate follow-ups.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** cron `wake` invoked by an agent inside a non-main session threads the originating session/agent through the wake gateway call so the system event lands on that session's queue (and `mode: "now"` drives the originating lane's heartbeat).
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, AJV-compiled schema validators, vitest 4.1.6.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run src/cron/service/wake-origin.test.ts src/gateway/protocol/index.test.ts`; `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.core.json`.
- **Evidence after fix:**

  ```
   Test Files  3 passed (3)
        Tests  35 passed (35)
     Duration  6.28s
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** `wake({mode, text, sessionKey, agentId})` calls `enqueueSystemEvent(text, {sessionKey, agentId})` and (on `mode: "now"`) `requestHeartbeatNow({reason: "wake", sessionKey, agentId})`. Omitted / whitespace-only origin falls through to the pre-fix default. Empty `text` still rejected.
- **What was not tested:** live end-to-end with a real Telegram thread → cron wake → delivery (exercises downstream delivery routing in the follow-up PR).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Closes the upstream half of #46886 and #64556.
- Related (downstream slices): #70268, #57199, #82767, #79869, #63096. This PR fixes the upstream origin-capture they all silently assume.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** existing in-process callers of `wake({mode, text})` keep working only if origin remains optional.
  - **Mitigation:** `sessionKey` / `agentId` optional; omitted (or whitespace-only) falls through to the dep's default. Regression test pins the branch.
- **Risk:** new `NonEmptyString` slots on the schema reject empty-string origin from existing callers.
  - **Mitigation:** existing callers don't send these fields. `additionalProperties: true` unchanged so forward-compat metadata still passes.
- **Risk:** inferred `agentId` could differ from a wake intended for a different agent.
  - **Mitigation:** explicit `sessionKey` / `agentId` params on the tool call override the inferred values. The implicit capture is the default for the "wake my own session" case.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** No.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — session keys and agent ids are not credentials.
- **New/changed network calls:** No.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `wake` action; only the routing target changes.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — wake events still confined to in-process session queues; routing now goes to the correct queue.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
